evidenceNotes: The current DDP exposes weekly bank-credit history from 1973 and current releases document Friday publication timing.
The online dated-release archive inspected reaches 2000, not the 60-month pre-May-1984 window required for Continental Illinois.
Current H.8 histories incorporate benchmark, seasonal-factor and underlying-data revisions, so they are not event-time vintages.
BIS documents narrow monthly coverage from 1964 and broad monthly coverage from 1994 as distinct regimes.
Time-varying three-year trade weights, chain linking and coverage expansions are documented and can be frozen as methodology regimes.
The portal exposes current reconstructed histories and release calendars, but provider-primary historical vintages for every frozen episode were not located.
BIS documents quarterly residence-based stocks, reporting-population breaks and FX- and break-adjusted changes.
The revisions-and-breaks summary and current methodology support a frozen regime manifest.
Immutable historical release snapshots and publication-date joins for every applicable episode were not established.
The FDIC data guide identifies annual aggregate historical data from 1934.
The current historical tool is retrospective and does not itself prove the values or definitions released before Continental Illinois.
The exact institution-count, concentration and failure/assistance asset-share component roster and immutable-snapshot terms remain unproven.
Z.1 amounts outstanding extend from 1945 and the Board publishes quarterly release dates and historical release files.
The online dated-release archive begins in September 1996, after Mexico bond turbulence in December 1994.
Current DDP history and later archives cannot prove release-available quarterly market values for the earliest frozen broad episode.
DGS2 is public-domain daily H.15 data with citation requested and history from June 1976.
ALFRED records series metadata and vintages only from June 28, 2005 for DGS2.
The required event-time snapshots for Mexico 1994 and Russia/LTCM 1998 are therefore not proven by ALFRED.
DGS10 is public-domain daily H.15 data with citation requested and long nominal coverage.
ALFRED records the DGS10 series from June 28, 2005, not the event-time vintages needed for the 1994 and 1998 episodes.
Current FRED values are the latest revision and cannot substitute for a missing historical snapshot.
DCD90 is public-domain daily H.15 data, was discontinued on June 28, 2013 and documents representative-response suppression.
FRED warns that all observations are subject to revision and its current page is not an August 1998 snapshot.
A provider-primary event-time vintage for Russia/LTCM was not proven without downloading historical observation-bearing releases.
The Treasury-bill reference leg retains the ready classification established by E14.7a under the same discount-basis semantics.
It is reused only as the matched reference leg and cannot make the paired DCD90 family ready by itself.
Weekly positions, transactions, financing and fails are available from January 28, 1998 and are split into six explicit data-structure regimes.
The provider documents Thursday publication with the previous week's statistics and warns that published data may reflect revisions.
Dated immutable releases for the 2019 and 2023 episodes and repository snapshot terms were not established.
